front: Film: American bar owner Rick Blaine helps ex-lover Ilsa Lund and her husband Victor Laszlo (Czech Resistance leader) escape Vichy-occupied Morocco using letters of transit
Film: Captain Renault (Claude Rains) tells his men to 'round up the usual suspects' after Major Strasser is killed
Film: at the climax, Rick tells Ilsa their problems 'don't amount to a hill of beans' before convincing her to board the plane with Victor Laszlo
Film: Rick directs a Bulgarian couple to try roulette number 22, earning the remark 'boss, you've done a beautiful thing'
Film: Major Strasser played by Conrad Veidt (also Cesare in The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari); reply to 'You despise me?' is 'If I gave you any thought, I probably would'
Film: closes with the line 'Louis, I think this is the beginning of a beautiful friendship'
Film: the imaginary letters of transit — documents needed by Ilsa and Victor Laszlo to flee German-controlled Africa to Lisbon
Film: a chorus of 'Die Wacht am Rhein' is countered by the band playing 'La Marseillaise'
Film: Captain Renault is shocked to find gambling at the café before being handed his winnings
Film: based on the play 'Everybody Comes to Rick's' by schoolteacher Murray Burnett and Joan Alison (called 'sophisticated hokum')
Film: Umberto Eco essay argues the film became a cult classic through 'intertextual archetypes' — an 'orgy of sacrificial archetypes' enabled by casting Conrad Veidt and Peter Lorre
Film: dwarf extras were used with fog to hide a cardboard model of the plane on the runway
Film: Sam (Dooley Wilson) is the Black pianist who is never actually told 'play it again'; Sam's piano playing was dubbed by Jean Plummer
Film: 'As Time Goes By' lyrics — 'You must remember this, a kiss is still a kiss, a sigh is just a sigh'; Ilsa asks Sam to play it; Sam pretends not to remember the melody
Film: producer Hal B. Wallis considered changing Sam's sex to cast Hazel Scott or Lena Horne; Epstein twins and Irene Diamond intervened
Film: scored by Max Steiner (Austrian 'father of film music'), who disliked 'As Time Goes By' but was required to use it; Steiner also scored King Kong and Gone with the Wind
Film: the 'round up the usual suspects' line gave its title to the 1995 film The Usual Suspects
